_meta.law: Stable Python lanes MUST be registered here as Rust-port candidates (CLAUDE.md doctrine). Do not delete entries — superseded entries get status=superseded.

candidates.reason: hot path: every corpus_chunk write goes through embed_text (BGE HTTP round-robin) + write_chunk (psycopg). ThreadPoolExecutor + threading.Lock already signal the pressure. OOM risk is managed by Python advisory env caps — Rust would own the ceiling natively. Highest throughput leverage.
blocking: BGE batch HTTP client (reqwest + async round-robin)
CAS streaming reader (cap enforced in type system, not env var)

candidates.reason: safety-critical custody layer: FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED dequeue, worker-contract gating, dead-letter writes. The queue spine is the authority boundary between operator intent and all downstream workers. Python GIL + startup latency add unnecessary jitter to every job cycle. Belongs in Rust alongside the rest of the custody/authority stack.
candidates.rust_target: lucidota_etl crate / absurd_queue module (or standalone absurd-spine binary)
blocking: absurd_worker_contracts.py gate logic -> Rust enum match
dead-letter + event schema mirroring in sqlx types
FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED idiom in sqlx

candidates.reason: 30s daemon loop reading /proc/pressure/{cpu,memory,io} + /proc/meminfo + nvidia-smi, deciding pressure rung, writing runtime_status_fact + governor_action to DB, actuating BGE fleet. Stable API (rung ladder is locked). A daemon this close to kernel telemetry and fleet actuation should be Rust: no GC pauses on the decision loop, direct /proc reads, typed rung enum.
candidates.rust_target: lucidota_etl crate / governor module (or standalone guvna binary)
blocking: nvidia-smi subprocess -> nvml-rs or raw sysfs

candidates.reason: batch-fill hot path for NULL embeddings: reads corpus_chunk rows in batches, fans out to BGE fleet via HTTP, execute_batch UPDATE back to storage DB. 28x speedup already proven at batch=32 vs single. Threading lock + random endpoint selection are load-bearing. Rust async (tokio + reqwest) would remove GIL contention on the HTTP fan-out and give typed batch control.
blocking: reqwest async batch HTTP to BGE fleet
batch sizing + backpressure in async stream

candidates.reason: streaming recursive archive ingest (~76GB corpus: zips, tars, nested). Bounded producer->Queue->worker pipeline keeps memory flat in Python, but the streaming + hashing + chunking + embed fan-out chain is exactly the Rust sweet spot: zero-copy streaming, typed archive enum (zip/tar/gz/7z), native backpressure via tokio channels. Episodic (not continuous) so p2 vs p0/p1 above.
blocking: recursive archive depth guard (type-safe depth counter)
